description: Canonical tool definitions for Den Memories runtime adapters (Hermes, pi-crew, etc.). Each adapter imports or generates tools from this file. The tool set available to a profile is determined by its tool_set config (agent or worker).

agent.description: Tools for non-worker agents. den_memory_store auto-promotes to curated memory. den_memory_propose is available for explicit candidate staging but agents should prefer den_memory_store.

worker.description: Tools for worker/subagent runtimes. Workers are metadata-only on capture, so they use den_memory_propose to create pending candidates. den_memory_store is excluded (it would be a no-op).

tools.name: den_memory_recall
tools.description: Read-only guided recall packet from Den Memories. Returns active curated memory entries ranked by relevance within the request scope. Auto-promoted captures are included. Profile/agent-scoped memories are discoverable within their scope.

scope_kind.description: Scope kind to filter by (project, profile, agent_instance, etc.)

tools.name: den_memory_read
tools.description: Read a single curated memory entry by its slug. Returns the full entry including body, metadata, scope, and source refs.

slug.description: Slug of the memory entry to read

tools.name: den_memory_search
tools.description: Full-text search across curated memory entries, optionally including pending candidates. Returns matching entries with title, summary, and scope.

tools.name: den_memory_store
tools.description: Store a memory. For non-worker agents: content is auto-promoted to an active curated memory entry immediately recall-visible within its scope. Profile/agent-scoped stores are particularly low-risk — store experimentation freely. Workers: use den_memory_propose instead (capture is metadata-only for workers).

raw_text.description: Raw text content to capture as a memory

scope_kind.description: Scope kind (project, profile, agent_instance, etc.). Profile/agent scopes are low-risk.

tools.name: den_memory_propose
tools.description: Propose a memory candidate for review. Creates a pending candidate only — no auto-promote. Use this when you want explicit curation review before the memory becomes recall-visible. Non-worker agents: prefer den_memory_store for immediate curated storage.

proposed_kind.description: Kind of memory (fact, observation, decision, etc.)

tools.name: den_memory_doctor
tools.description: Read-only Den Memories service health report. Returns schema version, migration status, and service state. Safe to call at any time.
